questions.q: 'Het is half drie' — what time is it?
questions.explanation: This is the #1 Dutch time trap! 'half drie' = halfway TO three = 2:30. NOT half past two (that would be half twee = 1:30).

questions.q: How do you say 'quarter past ten'?
questions.explanation: 'kwart over tien' = 10:15. 'kwart voor tien' = 9:45. Over = past. Voor = to/before.

questions.q: 'Het is kwart voor acht' — what time is it?
questions.explanation: 'kwart voor acht' = quarter TO eight = 7:45. 'voor' means before — quarter before eight.

questions.q: It's 2:25. How do you say this in Dutch?
questions.explanation: 2:25 = five minutes before 2:30 (half drie). Think: half drie = 2:30, so 2:25 = vijf voor half drie.

questions.q: Which verb is used for age in Dutch?
questions.explanation: Dutch uses 'zijn' for age: 'Ik BEN vijfendertig jaar.' Never 'ik heb 35 jaar' — that's a very common mistake!

questions.q: How do you say 'I am thirty-five years old'?
questions.explanation: 'Ik ben [number] jaar (oud).' The word 'oud' is optional — 'Ik ben 35 jaar' is perfectly natural.

options: In what year were you born?
questions.explanation: 'jarig zijn' = to have your birthday. You can also say 'Wanneer is jouw verjaardag?'

questions.q: 'Om hoe laat begint de les?' — what does 'om' signal?
options: It introduces a specific time point
questions.explanation: 'Om + time' = at [specific time]. 'Om twee uur' = at two o'clock. Always use 'om' before clock times.
